story.iWant: voice tool definitions stored as a seeded spec
story.soThat: tool descriptions can be updated without a code deploy and the tools are provider-agnostic by construction

context.applies: Every voice call that has tool enablement turned on in VoiceCallSettings. The VapiProvider adapter (lib/voice/providers/vapi) reads this spec at call-start via loadToolDefinitions() and embeds the active tools in the assistant config.
context.supersedes: VAPI_TOOL_DEFINITIONS TypeScript constant in app/api/vapi/tools/route.ts (removed in #1019)

function.name: lookup_teaching_point
function.description: Look up specific teaching content or facts about a topic. Use when the caller asks about a specific concept, rule, threshold, or definition.

topic.description: The topic or concept to look up

function.name: check_mastery
function.description: Check if the caller has mastered a specific module or concept. Use before deciding whether to teach new material or review.

module.description: The module or concept name to check

function.name: record_observation
function.description: Record an important observation about the caller during the conversation. Use when the caller reveals something significant about their knowledge, preferences, or situation.

function.name: get_practice_question
function.description: Get a practice question or scenario for the current topic. Use when transitioning to practice or assessment.

topic.description: The topic to get a practice question for

function.name: get_next_module
function.description: Find out what the next module or topic is in the caller's curriculum. Use when the current topic is mastered and you need to move on.

function.name: log_activity_result
function.description: Log the result of an interactive activity (pop quiz, MCQ, scenario, teach-back, etc.). Call this after completing any structured activity to track the outcome.

topic.description: The topic or concept the activity was about

notes.description: Brief notes on how the caller performed (e.g., 'got 4/5 in rapid fire', 'struggled with distinction between X and Y')

function.name: send_text_to_caller
function.description: Send a text message (SMS) to the caller. Use for multiple-choice questions with complex options, reflection prompts, follow-up resources, or anything better read than heard.

message.description: The text message content to send. Format with line breaks for readability.

purpose.description: The purpose of the text message

function.name: request_artifact
function.description: Request that a study artifact be sent to the caller after the call. Use when you want to share a summary, formula, exercise, study note, or resource with the learner. The artifact will be delivered after the call ends.

type.description: The type of artifact to send

title.description: A short descriptive title for the artifact (max 60 chars)

content.description: The content of the artifact in markdown format

reason.description: Why this artifact is being sent (for pipeline context)

function.name: share_content
function.description: Share a visual aid (image, diagram, PDF) with the caller. In text sim sessions, the content appears inline in the chat. In voice calls, the content is sent to the caller's phone via their preferred channel (WhatsApp, MMS, or SMS link). Use when discussing a figure, diagram, or document listed in your visual aids that the caller should see. Always describe the content verbally too.

media_id.description: The media ID from the visual aids list in your instructions

caption.description: Brief description of what the content shows and why you are sharing it

function.name: lookup_vocabulary
function.description: Look up the definition of a word or term from the course materials. Use when the caller asks "what does X mean?", encounters an unfamiliar word, or when you want to introduce key vocabulary. Returns the contextual definition from the source text, not a generic dictionary definition.

term.description: The word or term to look up
